fields.name: FY 2025 Actuals
fields.description: Full-year budget vs actuals for FY 2025. Post-Series-B scaling phase — fast on hiring, mixed on efficiency. Full year closed.

fields.name: FY 2026 Operating Plan
fields.description: Board-approved plan for FY 2026, built after the board asked for a credible path to 18-month runway. Headcount flat in engineering, two new AEs, marketing shifting toward content. Q1–Q2 actuals posted; Q3–Q4 are current forecast.

fields.notes: Three engineers at start of year; senior backend hired Feb 14. HR joked about the Valentine's Day onboarding. He did not find it funny.
Old and new data pipeline ran in parallel for six weeks during migration. AWS was delighted.
Standard renewals. Negotiated a 5% discount on the CI toolchain by threatening to look at alternatives. We were not actually looking at alternatives.
External security firm for pentest. Found 3 critical and 8 high-severity issues. Expensive. Less expensive than the alternative.
Split test: LinkedIn vs Google. LinkedIn CPL was 4x higher; pipeline quality was 3x better. The debate about what this means continued into Q2.
Four blog posts, one technical guide, zero viral moments. SEO rankings improving slowly, like a plant that doesn't know it's being watched.
FinTech Forum Frankfurt. Booth cost €12k. Leads generated: 4. Coffees consumed at the booth: estimated 60. ROI: contested.
Strong quarter. Two reps hit 120%+ of quota. Third rep reported 'strong pipeline' that did not close. Still reports strong pipeline.
Two reps flew to London for a UK enterprise on-site. Business class, approved after the fact. Deal closed. Finance said fine.
Salesforce + Outreach + Clay. Sales ops documented how they all connect in Notion. Three separate Notion pages, each slightly different.
Rent, utilities, coffee. The office coffee machine broke Feb 7. IT ticket opened Feb 7. Resolved Feb 8. Fastest ticket of the year.
Quarterly premium, professional liability and D&O. No incidents to report.
Two laptop refreshes: one for a new hire, one for a machine that had been 'almost broken' for four months.
Agency placed the senior backend engineer and one AE. 20% fee on both. Painful and fast, in that order.
Leadership development workshop planned. Facilitator cancelled four days before due to illness. Rescheduled to Q2. Budget fully unused.
Health and pension contributions. Also added a Headspace subscription for the whole team and called it a mental wellness initiative.
Full headcount. Lead engineer took parental leave in May; contractor covered at a daily rate that was, in retrospect, higher than the lead's own.
Reserved instance purchases dropped unit cost 18%. Engineering lead included it in the board deck under 'wins.' Rightfully so.
Added Datadog APM tier upgrade after the third incident where we first heard about downtime from a customer Slack message.
Mobile contractor was 'two weeks from done' for the entirety of Q2. Shipped in late June. App Store review took longer than the feature itself.
Pulled budget from display advertising. CTR was 0.04%. The banner creative was, in retrospect, not good.
Brought in a second freelancer for product-led content. Quality inconsistent but volume doubled.
Skipped external conferences. Ran two webinars instead. Combined attendance: 47. Qualified leads: 2. Everyone agreed this was normal.
Slow quarter. One AE went on a performance improvement plan. Another closed a €180k deal at 6:47pm on June 30 and saved his number.
One trip to Zurich that could have been a Zoom. In fairness, the prospect specifically asked us to come.
Added Gong for call recording. AEs claim they use it to prep for calls. Sales manager actually uses it.
HVAC broke in May. Repair took three weeks. Engineering wore hoodies. No one complained officially.
Quarterly premium. Finance asked if we could negotiate it lower. Broker said no.
Headsets for the sales team after customer complaints about background noise on calls. Immediate improvement across the board.
Head of Sales hired via referral from a board member. Zero agency fee. The internal referral bonus cost €2,000. Best €2,000 spent all year.
Leadership workshop finally ran. Eight managers attended. Workshop rated 6/10. The async Slack debrief after it rated 8/10. Draw your own conclusions.
No changes to the benefits package.
Mid-year raises effective July, averaging 6.2%. Two engineers had competing offers; both stayed. It was closer than anyone admitted.
Stable. The kind of quarter that briefly makes the CFO feel things are under control.
No changes to the stack. Celebrated by not talking about it.
External auditor brought in for SOC 2 Type II prep. Spent more time in the company Slack than most employees. Found several gaps we had been meaning to fix since 2023.
Doubled down on LinkedIn after finally settling the Q1 debate. Pipeline responded. The person who argued against it has gone quiet.
'The Compliance Iceberg' long-form guide generated 40 demo requests. Nobody predicted this. Everyone claims they did.
First customer summit, Berlin. 38 attendees. One deal that had gone dark six months ago reopened at dinner. That one deal alone covered the event cost.
New AE closed her first solo deal in week 9 of ramp, two weeks ahead of schedule. Bought the team lunch. Good sign.
Conference season: flights, hotels, client dinners. One deal started at a hotel bar in Amsterdam at 11pm. Pipeline is where you find it.
The integration between Salesforce and our data warehouse broke silently in August. Discovered in September. Six weeks of bad forecasting data. Fixed.
Nothing broke. Under budget. Noted for the record.
Mid-year renewal. Premium up 7%. Broker said it was 'the market.' We said okay.
Three laptops for new hires. Also replaced the office router, which had been running hot since early 2022 and which everyone had been meaning to report.
PM role open since April. Final candidate was the third person who applied in week one. Search took five months.
Team offsite in Leipzig. Go-karting, a strategy session, and a long dinner. First offsite in 18 months. Morale measurably improved for approximately three weeks.
One employee formally asked about dental coverage. HR said 'good point, let's revisit in planning.'
Staff engineer resigned Nov 12 for a FAANG offer. Partial month saved on payroll. Cost: six months of institutional knowledge and one very uncomfortable all-hands.
Year-end compliance processing for three enterprise clients hit simultaneously in December. The AWS bill arrived on Dec 28. CFO replied with a single emoji.
Added Sentry and LaunchDarkly. Both should have been purchased in 2024. Better late than after the next incident.
No contractors in Q4. Team held together without external help. Noted with appreciation.
Year-end blitz across all channels. Two enterprise deals influenced by marketing touchpoints. Attribution remains a lively topic between marketing and sales.
Year-in-review and 2026 predictions content series. One post picked up by a fintech newsletter with 12k subscribers. CMO screenshotted the open rate email.
Client appreciation dinner in Munich. 12 customers, excellent food, two renewals confirmed on the walk back to the hotel.
Three reps at 140%+ of quota. Highest commission payout of the year. Finance described it as a 'good problem to have,' visibly through clenched teeth.
Holiday season. Fewer flights, more client dinners. Net spend under budget.
Stack unchanged. A small miracle heading into year-end.
New standing desks for engineering, monitor refresh for sales, deep clean. All approved in the final week of the year under the 'use it or lose it' budget logic.
New quarterly rate following the mid-year renewal. Accepted without drama.
Year-end equipment refresh. Monitors and peripherals for three new hires onboarding in January.
Backfill for the departed staff engineer plus a new SDR role. The SDR role was approved by the CEO via voice memo at 11pm on a Tuesday.
Conference budget mostly unused. Two engineers attended an AWS summit and expensed it separately under 'cloud education.' Budget carried into 2026 planning.
Dental coverage not added. Planning noted it for 2026. As predicted.
Headcount freeze holds at 16 engineers. Two turned down outside offers in January. Retention holding. Reasons: unclear, probably RSA vesting.
Reserved instances plus architecture cleanup delivered real savings. First quarter under €70k in two years. Engineering shared a celebratory meme in #infra.
Datadog contract renegotiated flat. Linear added after an engineering vote: 9 in favour, 1 opposed. The one dissenter now uses it daily and says nothing.
No contractors needed. Team managed the full roadmap internally for the first time in 18 months.
Intentionally slowed spend while testing a new ICP message. CPL improved 22% vs Q4. Volume down, quality up. CMO is describing this as a strategy.
Content team now 2 FTEs plus one freelancer. Output rate doubled vs 2025. Organic traffic up 34% year-over-year. The SEO plant is finally growing.
Small founder dinner in Amsterdam. 12 prospects, good conversation, two deals in pipeline within 30 days. Cheaper and better than the Frankfurt booth.
Two new AEs started January 6. Both in ramp period. Below quota, above expectations for week eight. Head of Sales called it 'encouraging.'
Three enterprise on-sites: one Paris, two Amsterdam. All produced a clear next step. Travel budget under control for once.
Added Clay for intent-based prospecting. SDR pipeline quality jumped noticeably by week three. Several people are taking credit.
Lease renewed at the same rate. Also negotiated two additional parking spots. The ops team is quietly proud of this.
Standard premium. Cyber liability coverage quietly increased ahead of renewal conversations with enterprise prospects who ask about it.
New AE spilled an entire coffee onto his MacBook Pro on day three. Replaced under AppleCare at zero cost. The story has been told at every all-hands since.
Sales referral program paid out twice in Q1. Net saving vs agency fee: approximately €8k. Head of Sales takes full credit.
Leadership program delivered without incident. Managers rated it 8.2 out of 10. Marked improvement from the 6/10 in Q2 2025. Same content, different facilitation.
Dental plan added effective January 1. It only took two years of 'good point, let's revisit in planning.'
One engineer on a four-week unpaid sabbatical in June. Covered internally without a contractor. Actual came in under budget.
New async data processing feature spiked compute in May. Costs up slightly, but the feature is now the most requested on the roadmap. Good spike.
Stack stable. No additions, no removals. Q2 2026 is already a better year for software discipline than all of 2025.
Critical bug in the compliance validation module surfaced in production in May. External specialist brought in for three weeks. Not in the plan. Fixed in 18 days.
DACH region campaign launched. German-language ads underperformed significantly; Dutch-market ads outperformed. Adjusted mid-quarter. Learning: always test Dutch first.
Added a video producer for customer case studies. First video published June 3, 1,200 views in the first week. More than any blog post in 2025.
Money20/20 Europe: small sponsorship package. Good badge scans, better hallway conversations. Two pilots opened from conference contacts.
Both AEs out of ramp. One closed a €220k deal in June — the largest single deal in company history. Announced in #general with a gif that divided opinion.
DACH expansion meant six enterprise on-sites across Zurich, Munich, and Frankfurt in eight weeks. Reps complained about the travel. All three Zurich deals have next steps.
New sales manager runs weekly Gong call reviews. AEs have improved measurably on discovery and objection handling. Causation suspected but not proven.
New espresso machine installed (Italian brand, espresso-only). Initial complaints from the filter coffee contingent. Both factions now coexist peacefully.
No changes.
Headset upgrade for the customer support function. Previous headsets were described internally as 'technically working.'
Senior marketing hire via agency after a 10-week search. The first internal candidate backed out two days before the offer. The agency one worked out better anyway.
Manager onboarding for two newly promoted team leads, using curriculum built from the Q1 offsite. Under budget, high-rated.
Dental plan usage tracking begins Q3. Adoption appears high based on zero complaints, which is how dental benefits work.
Headcount stable. One offer from market expected; retention budget reserved but not communicated to the team. (Forecast)
Targeting an additional 5% reduction via spot instances for non-critical workloads. CFO has circled this line item in the model. (Forecast)
No changes planned. (Forecast)
Year-end security review budgeted proactively. Learning from Q3 2025: plan for the auditor, not against them. (Forecast)
Scaling what worked in Q2. No new experiments planned. This is either discipline or lack of imagination; TBD. (Forecast)
Six new guides in production. Compound SEO effect from 2025–2026 content expected to continue accelerating. (Forecast)
One customer roundtable planned, no large conferences. Keeping it intimate based on Q1 Amsterdam dinner results. (Forecast)
Both AEs at full quota expectation. Pipeline coverage at 3.2x entering Q3. Cautiously optimistic is the official position. (Forecast)
DACH and UK focus continuing. Budget sized for eight enterprise on-sites. (Forecast)
Evaluating Clari for revenue forecasting. Decision expected Q3 or Q4. (Forecast)
(Forecast)
D&O policy expansion planned ahead of anticipated Series C conversations in H2. Broker's recommendation; accepted without debate. (Forecast)
(Forecast)
No planned hires. Placeholder budget for opportunistic referrals only. (Forecast)
Team offsite planned for September. Location still being decided. Engineering has voted for somewhere with hiking. Sales has voted for somewhere with a spa. (Forecast)
Dental usage data expected from insurer. First cohort data on a benefits investment we can actually measure. (Forecast)
Year-end. Possible engineering hire if Series C closes earlier than expected. Unbudgeted but flagged in the model footnotes. (Forecast)
Year-end enterprise processing load expected again. Reserved capacity pre-purchased this time. We learned from December 2025. (Forecast)
GitHub Enterprise annual renewal expected. Budget includes a small buffer for the inevitable Datadog conversation. (Forecast)
Security review budgeted proactively. Also a small buffer in case the Series C due diligence process triggers a technical audit. (Forecast)
Year-end pipeline push. 15% increase over Q3. If Q3 content compounds as expected, paid should convert better than 2025. (Forecast)
Year-in-review series planned. Also a 2027 benchmark report, which has become the highest-converting asset type in the library. (Forecast)
Holiday client dinners only. Intimate format. The Munich dinner in Q4 2025 set a high bar. (Forecast)
If Q3 pipeline converts at historical rates, commissions will exceed plan. Finance has modelled this scenario and has a face for it. (Forecast)
Holiday slowdown expected. Budget conservative relative to Q2–Q3. Client dinners will dominate. (Forecast)
Clari likely added if Q3 evaluation goes well. Included in Q4 budget as a line item, not yet committed. (Forecast)
Year-end refresh budgeted conservatively. No standing desk experiments this time. (Forecast)
(Forecast)
Year-end refresh for engineering. Also: a proper coffee-proof laptop sleeve has been procured for all new sales hires. (Forecast)
Placeholder for one engineering hire if Series C closes ahead of schedule. Not committed. Flagged by the CEO as 'aspirational.' (Forecast)
Conference budget allocated with intentional flexibility. Lesson from 2025: don't carry it unused into January. (Forecast)
Dental plan fully operational for a full year. Will run an employee satisfaction survey. Fully expect it to be the highest-rated benefit added since the coffee machine. (Forecast)
